summary_en: Matsuya R&D's board reaffirms support for Omron Healthcare's tender offer to acquire the company as a wholly-owned subsidiary at ¥1,110 per share, with a minimum purchase of 11.23M shares and planned delisting.
title_en: Notice of Support Opinion and Recommendation to Tender in Response to Omron Healthcare Co., Ltd.'s Tender Offer for Our Company's Shares and Share Subscription Rights
translation_en_brief:
On 18 May 2026, Matsuya R&D Co., Ltd. announced that its board of directors has reaffirmed its support for an all-cash tender offer by Omron Healthcare Co., Ltd. to acquire the company as a wholly-owned subsidiary. The tender offer, which commenced on 19 May 2026, runs for 20 business days until 15 June 2026 (extendable to 30 business days under certain conditions).

The offer price is ¥1,110 per ordinary share and ¥717,600 per share subscription right. The minimum number of shares to be acquired is 11,230,300 shares, which would result in Omron Healthcare holding 66.57% of outstanding shares post-transaction. Omron Healthcare currently owns 14.64% of Matsuya R&D and is the parent company of the bidder. The board's position, reconfirmed after review, is based on a special committee's recommendations and supports the transaction premise that the company will be delisted and become a wholly-owned subsidiary of Omron Healthcare.
